conditions.physical_address: Department of Chemistry, Indiana University, Bloomington, Indiana 47405
conditions.evidence_text: Adsorption Chromatography at Critical Conditions of Adsorption (LACCC) for Off-Line Fractionation. The following system was employed (BAM laboratories): column, 2 X YMC RP 18 (150, 300 Å); eluents, methanol/water 83/17 (v/v); detection, ELSD, SEDEX 45 (Sedere, ERC); temperature, 45 ° C; flow rate, 0.5 mL min[-][1] ; concentration, 5 samples PEO (2-6), total amount, 5.41 mg mL[-][1] ; injection amount, 10 and 1 _µ_ L (Scheme 1, LACCC chromatogram).
conditions.critical_condition_basis: liquid adsorption chromatography at critical conditions
conditions.notes: The text explicitly states LACCC separates components differing by end groups, implying the PEO backbone is at the critical condition.
